[ROLE_DEFINITION]  
The inventory manager must assign new positions to containers based on priority, ensuring high-priority containers are near the entrance (0, 0) and minimizing total movement cost using Manhattan distance.

[CONSTRAINTS]  
Containers with higher priority must be placed closer to (0, 0). Movement cost is calculated as |x_i - x'_i| + |y_i - y'_i|. All positions must be on a 10x10 grid. No commas allowed in response.

[OUTPUT_FORMAT]  
Modules must be labeled, self-contained, and follow exact formatting rules (no periods, consistent capitalization, start and end with "Education").

[ERROR_PREVENTION]  
Avoid overlapping modules, ensure constraints are applied, and verify all requirements are met. Use precise language and avoid ambiguity.

[MODULE_1]  
Verify that containers with higher priority are placed closer to (0, 0). C_1 (10) at (2, 3) → (0, 0). C_2 (8) at (5, 1) → (1, 0). C_3 (7) at (9, 4) → (0, 1). C_4 (5) at (3, 8) → (1, 1). C_5 (3) at (6, 6) → (0, 2). All priorities are respected.

[MODULE_2]  
Calculate movement cost for each container. C_1: |2-0| + |3-0| = 5. C_2: |5-1| + |1-0| = 5. C_3: |9-0| + |4-1| = 13. C_4: |3-1| + |8-1| = 6. C_5: |6-0| + |6-2| = 10. Total movement cost: 5 + 5 + 13 + 6 + 10 = 39.

[MODULE_3]
